10	// cmdSetProcessGroup configures cmd so that it and all its children share a
11	// new process group (Setpgid) and are automatically killed when the parent
12	// thread dies (Pdeathsig). This ensures that if the parent worker dies
13	// abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM), the harness child is reaped by the kernel
14	// rather than orphaning to PID 1.
15	//
16	// Pdeathsig is sent when the creating thread dies, not the process, so the
17	// caller must use runtime.LockOSThread around cmd.Start() to prevent Go
18	// runtime from migrating the goroutine to a different OS thread. See
19	// golang.org/issue/27505 for details.
20	//
21	// Note: Pdeathsig is Linux-only. macOS and Windows do not support it; the
22	// graceful shutdown path (cmdKillProcessGroup on context cancellation) serves
23	// as the fallback for those platforms, and the orphan reaper is the final
24	// backstop for already-dead workers.
25	func cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
26		cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{
27			Setpgid:   true,
28			Pdeathsig: syscall.SIGKILL,
29		}
30	}
31	
32	// cmdKillProcessGroup sends SIGKILL to the entire process group of cmd.
33	// Because cmd was started with Setpgid=true, its PID equals its PGID, so
34	// passing -pid kills all descendants that inherited the group.
35	func cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
36		if cmd.Process == nil {
37			return
38		}
39		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
40	}

17	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
18	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
19	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
20	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
21	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
22	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
23		cmd := &cobra.Command{
24			Use:   "work",
25			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
26			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
27	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
28	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
29	
30	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
31	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
32	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
33	bounds.
34	
35	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
36	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
37	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
38	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
39	
40	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
41	  drained     — no ready beads remain
42	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
43	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
44	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
45	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
46	
47	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
48	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
49	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
50	`,
51			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
52	  ddx work
53	
54	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
55	  ddx work --once
56	
57	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
58	  ddx work --watch
59	
60	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
61	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
62	
63	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
64	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
65	
66	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
67	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
68	
69	  # Constrain power powerClass (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
70	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
71			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
72			RunE: f.runWork,
73		}
74	
75		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
76		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
77		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
78		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")

A parent-death signal is not sufficient as the only guard: existing orphaned execute-bead harness trees from prior dead workers can remain under PID 1, and platforms without Linux Pdeathsig need a startup backstop. `ddx work` needs to clean up orphaned harness children from prior dead workers without killing harnesses owned by live workers or unrelated projects.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 starts harness children for execute-bead attempts, but cleanup is tied to the current parent process running stopProcess.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 can kill a process group only when called by a live executor path.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 creates a signal.NotifyContext for graceful signals, but there is no `ddx work` startup scan for orphaned execute-bead harness processes whose owner PID is gone.
- The parent bead identifies the needed integration with lease-release/operator-attention behavior as cross-ref ddx-8f2e0ebf, but no startup reaper currently adopts that responsibility.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a `ddx work` startup reaper that identifies execute-bead harness processes from prior dead workers, scoped to the current project/workspace using durable ownership evidence rather than broad command-name matching alone.
- Kill only orphaned process groups whose recorded owner PID is gone or whose parent is PID 1 and whose workspace/attempt metadata ties them to the current project.
- Preserve live-owned harnesses, including other concurrent workers in the same project.
- Record or surface operator-attention evidence compatible with the existing lease-release/operator-attention machinery when orphan cleanup occurs.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add Linux Pdeathsig handling in this child; that is covered by the executor parent-death child bead.
- Do not change docker-clone process/container cleanup; that is covered by the docker-clone child bead.
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child remains at the ddx orchestrator -> harness-child boundary.
